Introduction

#> [1] "Trawl data imported"

Data summaries:

How many ‘bad’ coordinates do we have? Either missing or with minute coordinates greater than 60.

1:

cno
1 2017001
10 2018001
25 2022006

How many missing weights?

19

Missing weights with no corresponding length measurements?

19

Length-mass relationships

While directly measured weights are present for most species, some of the data are censored due the precision of the balance, so weights bottom out at 0.01 and, for small species, exhibit distinct ‘steps’. This creates an artificial truncation of the lower bound of the data and can also influence biomass estimates. This is likely not a huge deal for the purposes here, but if we want to incorporate this into any analyses, we can model masses and their uncertainty by incorporating the censored data structure directly into a length-mass model.

#> $Alewife
